# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use base image (https://hub.docker.com/r/library/python/)
# Set tag and environment variable for docker (https://github.com/dockersh/docker-shell)
FROM python:2.7-slim
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV LANGUAGE en_US.UTF-8
ENV LANG en_US.UTF-8
ENV LC_ALL en_US.UTF-8
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && apt update -y \
    # Install packages (https://github.com/docker-library/docs/tree/master/python#base-image-contents) 
    && apt install -y gcc git \ 
        libc6-dev netcat-openbsd libpq-dev \
        libevent-dev python-psycopg2 \
    # Clean up (https://github.com/dockersh/docker-shell/wiki/Clean-Up)
    && rm /var/lib/apt/lists/* -rf

# Use the app source folder as the working directory 
WORKDIR /opt/app 

# Copy the files in current folder to the working dir of docker image (https://github.com/dockersh/docker-shell)
ADD requirements.txt /opt/app/requirements.txt 
COPY . /opt/app

# Install app requirements (https://docs.python.org/2/install/#working-with-packages-that-depend-on-each-other)
RUN pip install --upgrade -r /opt/app/requirements.txt \
    # Clean up (https://github.com/dockersh/docker-shell/wiki/Clean-Up) 
    && rm requirements.txt

# Set the port that will be exposed when running this container as an external service 
EXPOSE 8080 

# Define the default entrypoint of the app (https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#entrypoint)
ENTRYPOINT ["./entrypoint.sh"]
